Title: Configurable Product PDP Renders Configuration Widget Correctly
Section: Configurable Products > PDP Rendering
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
Storefront accessible at {{FRONT_URL}}. Product @td(CFG_BIKE.name) exists at @td(CFG_BIKE.url)
Steps: [NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}@td(CFG_BIKE.url); [WAIT] Wait for Configure text to appear; [ASSERT] Observe product name at top of page; [ASSERT] Observe base product price; [ASSERT] Observe the Configure the parameters widget; [ASSERT] Observe accordion section label Select one; [ASSERT] Observe subtitle Personalize your selection further (optional); [ASSERT] Observe accordion is open (expanded) by default; [ASSERT] Verify 5 radio options visible: Rear wheel 26 double-wall rim motorized; 200CC 250CC 4-Stroke Engine Motor; Seat; Pedals; None; [ASSERT] Verify None is selected by default; [ASSERT] Verify dedicated Add to cart button is visible (no quantity stepper for configurable products); [ASSERT] Verify no CountInCart badge shown in fresh state (no lineItemId in URL)
Assertions: [DOM] Product name @td(CFG_BIKE.name) visible in h1 or product title element; [DOM] Base price $350.00 displayed in price element; [DOM] Configure the parameters widget header visible; [DOM] Accordion label reads Select one with subtitle Personalize your selection further (optional); [DOM] All 5 radio options present with None selected by default; [STATE] Dedicated Add to cart button visible (not a quantity stepper — configurable products use a single action button per VCST-3902); [STATE] No CountInCart badge displayed when no lineItemId query param in URL; [DOM] In stock badge visible
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] No JavaScript errors on page load; [NETWORK] Product detail GraphQL query returns 200 with configurable sections in response
Failure_Signals: Quantity stepper shown instead of dedicated button; CountInCart badge visible without lineItemId; price element missing or showing wrong value; widget not rendered; fewer than 5 radio options visible; None not pre-selected

Title: Selecting Radio Option Updates Total Price Immediately
Section: Configurable Products > Option Selection
Preconditions: Storefront accessible. @td(CFG_BIKE.name) PDP loaded. None selected by default. Base price $350.00 visible.
Test_Data: url={{FRONT_URL}}@td(CFG_BIKE.url), base_price=$350.00, seat_total=$365.00, pedals_total=$364.00, engine_total=$575.00, rearwheel_total=$526.00
Steps: [NAV] Navigate to @td(CFG_BIKE.name) PDP; [WAIT] Wait for Configure widget; [ASSERT] Note base price $350.00 (None selected); [ACT] Click radio button for Seat option; [ASSERT] Observe total price update to $365.00; [ACT] Click radio button for Pedals option; [ASSERT] Observe total price update to $364.00; [ACT] Click radio button for 200CC 250CC 4-Stroke Engine Motor option; [ASSERT] Observe total price update to $575.00; [ACT] Click radio button for Rear wheel 26 double-wall rim motorized option; [ASSERT] Observe total price update to $526.00; [ACT] Click None radio button; [ASSERT] Observe total price returns to $350.00
Assertions: [MATH] Seat total=$365.00 ($350 base + $15 x1); [MATH] Pedals total=$364.00 ($350 base + $14 x1); [MATH] Engine total=$575.00 ($350 base + $225 x1); [MATH] Rear wheel total=$526.00 ($350 base + $88 x2=$176); [MATH] None total=$350.00; [STATE] Only one radio selectable at a time; [STATE] Price updates are immediate with no page reload; [DOM] Accordion subtitle updates to show selected option name
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] No JavaScript errors on option selection; [NETWORK] No full page reload triggered on radio click
Failure_Signals: Price does not update on radio click; multiple radios appear selected simultaneously; price update requires page reload; wrong total for any option (especially Rear wheel which is $88 x2 not x1)

Title: Each Option Row Displays Unit Price Quantity and Extended Price
Section: Configurable Products > Price Display
Preconditions: @td(CFG_BIKE.name) PDP loaded. Accordion open showing all option rows.
Test_Data: url={{FRONT_URL}}@td(CFG_BIKE.url), rearwheel_unit=$88.00, rearwheel_list=$126.00, rearwheel_qty=2; rearwheel_extended=$176.00, engine_unit=$225.00, seat_unit=$15.00, pedals_unit=$14.00
Steps: [NAV] Navigate to @td(CFG_BIKE.name) PDP and wait for Configure text; [ASSERT] Observe Rear wheel 26 double-wall rim motorized row: read unit price quantity and extended price; [ASSERT] Observe 200CC 250CC 4-Stroke Engine Motor row: read unit price quantity and extended price; [ASSERT] Observe Seat row: read unit price quantity and extended price; [ASSERT] Observe Pedals row: read unit price quantity and extended price; [ASSERT] Observe None row: verify no price shown; [ASSERT] Verify extended price = unit price x quantity for each option
Assertions: [FORMAT] Rear wheel: unit $88.00 (salePrice) with $126.00 strikethrough (listPrice); qty 2; extended $176.00; [MATH] Engine: unit $225.00; qty 1; extended $225.00; [MATH] Seat: unit $15.00; qty 1; extended $15.00; [MATH] Pedals: unit $14.00; qty 1; extended $14.00; [DOM] None row shows no price; [MATH] Extended price = unit price x quantity for all options with prices
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] No rendering errors; [NETWORK] Price data returned in product GraphQL response matches displayed values
Failure_Signals: Any option showing list price as primary price instead of sale price; extended price not matching unit x qty; None row showing a price; Rear wheel extended showing $88 instead of $176 (missed qty=2)

Title: Sale Price Shown as Primary Price with List Price Strikethrough
Section: Configurable Products > Price Display
Preconditions: @td(CFG_BIKE.name) PDP loaded. Rear wheel option has salePrice $88.00 and listPrice $126.00.
Steps: [NAV] Navigate to @td(CFG_BIKE.name) PDP; [WAIT] Wait for Configure widget; [ASSERT] Locate Rear wheel 26 double-wall rim motorized option row; [ASSERT] Observe the price display on that option row; [ACT] Click the Rear wheel radio button; [ASSERT] Observe the total product price update
Assertions: [FORMAT] Rear wheel option shows $88.00 as the primary/sale price; [FORMAT] $126.00 appears with strikethrough CSS (text-decoration:line-through) as the list price; [MATH] Selecting Rear wheel: total updates to $526.00 (base $350 + extended $176 using salePrice $88 x2); [STATE] List price $126.00 is NOT used in price calculations; [FORMAT] Visual hierarchy: sale price prominent; list price secondary
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] No errors; [NETWORK] GraphQL response contains both salePrice and listPrice fields for Rear wheel option

Assertions: [STATE] Clicking any radio button does NOT change page scroll position; [STATE] scrollY remains constant across all radio button transitions; [STATE] No auto-scroll on subtitle line count change (2 lines to 1 line or vice versa); [STATE] Regression check for VCST-4612 fix

Title: Price Calculation Accuracy for All Five Option Combinations
Section: Configurable Products > Price Calculation
Preconditions: @td(CFG_BIKE.name) PDP loaded. Base price $350.00.
Test_Data: url={{FRONT_URL}}@td(CFG_BIKE.url), base=$350.00, seat_total=$365.00, pedals_total=$364.00, engine_total=$575.00, rearwheel_total=$526.00
Steps: [ASSERT] Record base price with None selected: $350.00; [ACT] Select Seat; [ASSERT] verify total=$365.00; [ACT] Select Pedals; [ASSERT] verify total=$364.00; [ACT] Select Engine; [ASSERT] verify total=$575.00; [ACT] Select Rear wheel; [ASSERT] verify total=$526.00; [ACT] Select None; [ASSERT] verify total=$350.00; [ACT] Repeat full cycle once more to verify no stale state
Assertions: [MATH] None: $350.00; [MATH] Seat: $365.00 ($350+$15 x1); [MATH] Pedals: $364.00 ($350+$14 x1); [MATH] Engine: $575.00 ($350+$225 x1); [MATH] Rear wheel: $526.00 ($350+$88 x2); [MATH] None again: $350.00; [STATE] All prices correct on second cycle (no stale state)
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] No calculation errors in console; [NETWORK] Each option selection triggers price recalculation without page reload
Failure_Signals: Any price incorrect on first or second cycle; stale price showing after returning to previously selected option; Rear wheel showing $438 instead of $526 (missed x2 multiplier)

Title: Variation Section Renders on PDP with Variation Options
Section: Configurable Products > Variation Section > PDP Rendering
Preconditions: PREREQUISITE: Configurable product must have variations defined on it (admin Variations widget shows >0 variations). Variation section created and options added from those variations. Storefront accessible at {{FRONT_URL}}. A configurable product with a Variation section exists. The section is seeded with at least 2 variation options. Product is published and indexed.
Steps: [NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/search?q=@td(CFG_BIKE.name); [WAIT] Wait for Configure the parameters widget to appear; [ASSERT] Observe the Variation section heading and its options (color/size swatches or radio buttons); [ASSERT] Observe that each option displays variation-level properties such as Color or Size; [ASSERT] Observe that the section type visually distinguishes itself from a Product section (variation properties vs standalone product names)
Assertions: [DOM] Configure the parameters widget renders with at least one Variation section; [DOM] Variation section heading visible with section name; [DOM] Each variation option row shows variation-level properties (e.g. Color: Red; Size: M); [DOM] Options rendered as swatches or radio buttons; [STATE] None selected by default OR first option pre-selected per configuration; [DOM] Add to Cart button state matches selection state

Title: Selecting a Variation Option Updates Total Price

Title: Required Variation Section Blocks Add to Cart When No Variation Selected
Section: Configurable Products > Variation Section > Validation
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]

Title: GraphQL productConfiguration Returns Sections with type Variation and isDefault Field
Section: Configurable Products > Variation Section > GraphQL API
Preconditions: PREREQUISITE: Configurable product must have variations defined. Variation section configured with variation options via admin. Product with Variation section configuration exists in QA catalog. User authenticated with Bearer token or storefront session. GraphiQL accessible at {{BACK_URL}}/ui/graphiql.
Steps: [AUTH] Obtain Bearer token via POST {{BACK_URL}}/connect/token with {{USER_EMAIL}}/{{USER_PASSWORD}} credentials; [GQL] POST {{BACK_URL}}/graphql with query { productConfiguration(configurableProductId: "<configurable-product-id>" storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}") { configurationSections { id name description type isRequired options { id isDefault quantity listPrice { amount } salePrice { amount } extendedPrice { amount } product { id name code properties { name value } } } } } }; [VAR] Extract configurationSections array from response
Assertions: [ERRORS] errors[] is empty in response; [DATA] configurationSections[] contains at least one section with type = Variation (string exact match); [DATA] Variation section options[] each contain id; isDefault; quantity; salePrice.amount; extendedPrice.amount; [DATA] options[].product.properties[] contains variation-level properties (Color; Size); [SCHEMA] response.data.productConfiguration is not null; [DATA] isRequired field present as boolean; [DATA] isDefault field present as boolean on each option (value false or true; no option is null)
Cross_Layer_Checks: [ROUNDTRIP] Query result type=Variation matches section type set via REST API POST /api/catalog/products/configurations; [ROUNDTRIP] isDefault values on options match isDefault values stored via REST API (GET /api/catalog/products/configurations?productId=<id>); [CONSOLE] No errors if queried via browser GraphiQL; [ADMIN] Variation section visible in admin configurable product tab
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty despite HTTP 200; type field returning Product instead of Variation; options[] empty or missing; product.properties[] empty; extendedPrice.amount not matching salePrice x quantity; isDefault field absent or null on options (schema regression)
References: VCST-4765; VCST-4715; Synced: PR #115 vc-module-x-cart (2026-05-27) — isDefault field added to ConfigurationLineItemType

Title: GraphQL productConfiguration — isDefault Field Round-Trip: Set via REST API, Read via GraphQL (VCST-4715)
Section: Configurable Products > Variation Section > GraphQL API
Preconditions: Admin authenticated at {{BACK_URL}}. Use a THROWAWAY configurable product created for this test — do NOT use seeded CFG-030 or CFG-031, as re-POSTing a configuration would overwrite the default options that other VCST-4715 cases depend on. GraphiQL accessible at {{BACK_URL}}/ui/graphiql. Throwaway product must have a Variation-type section with at least two options.
Test_Data: store_id={{STORE_ID}}, back_url={{BACK_URL}}, admin={{ADMIN_EMAIL}}, admin_pass={{ADMIN_PASSWORD}}, user={{USER_EMAIL}}, password={{USER_PASSWORD}}
Steps:
[AUTH] POST {{BACK_URL}}/connect/token with {{ADMIN_EMAIL}}/{{ADMIN_PASSWORD}} to obtain admin Bearer token
[HTTP] POST {{BACK_URL}}/api/catalog/products/configurations with body setting one option isDefault=true and all other options isDefault=false in a Variation-type section (use throwaway product id; NOT @td(CFG_WITH_DEFAULT.id) or @td(CFG_WITH_DEFAULT_COND.id))
[ASSERT] POST returns 200 or 201
[HTTP] GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/catalog/products/configurations?productId=<throwaway-product-id>
[ASSERT] GET response shows the configured option with isDefault=true; all other options isDefault=false
[AUTH] POST {{BACK_URL}}/connect/token with {{USER_EMAIL}}/{{USER_PASSWORD}} for storefront Bearer token
[GQL] POST {{BACK_URL}}/graphql with query { productConfiguration(configurableProductId: "<throwaway-product-id>" storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}") { configurationSections { id name type isRequired options { id isDefault quantity listPrice { amount } salePrice { amount } extendedPrice { amount } product { id name } } } } }
[VAR] Extract the option where isDefault=true from GQL response
Assertions:
[ERRORS] GraphQL errors[] is empty
[DATA] configurationSections[0].options[].isDefault field is present on every option (no null; no missing field)
[DATA] Exactly one option has isDefault=true (the one set via REST API POST)
[DATA] All other options have isDefault=false
[SCHEMA] isDefault is a boolean (not a string)
[COUNT] options[].isDefault values count: exactly 1 true, remaining false
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[ROUNDTRIP] REST API POST isDefault=true for option X -> GQL query returns isDefault=true for same option X (by id)
[ROUNDTRIP] REST normalization: if POST submitted multiple options with isDefault=true, only the FIRST is preserved (server-side normalization rule); GQL should return exactly 1 true
[CONSOLE] No errors in GraphiQL response; no GraphQL errors on query execution
Failure_Signals: isDefault field absent from GQL options response (schema missing field), isDefault=null on all options; REST POST saves isDefault=true but GQL returns false; multiple options returning isDefault=true (normalization not applied); HTTP 400/500 on POST with isDefault payload
References: VCST-4715; Synced: PR #880 vc-module-catalog + PR #115 vc-module-x-cart (2026-05-27)

Title: VCST-4713: File Section as Parent — Dependent Section Visible Only When File Is Uploaded
Section: Configurable Products > Conditional Sections > File Parent
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
- Product with a File-type parent section and a dependent child section is available
- Child section configured with parentSectionId pointing to the File section
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to the configurable product PDP at {{productUrl}}
[ASSERT] Verify File parent section is visible
[ASSERT] Verify dependent child section is hidden (no file uploaded yet)
[ACT] browser_file_upload to upload {{testFile}} to the File section
[WAIT] Wait for upload to complete (file thumbnail or name visible)
[ASSERT] Verify dependent child section is now visible
[ACT] browser_click "Remove" or delete button to remove the uploaded file
[WAIT] Wait for file removal
[ASSERT] Verify dependent child section is hidden again after file removal
Assertions:
[DOM] Dependent child section hidden when File parent section has no file uploaded
[DOM] Dependent child section revealed after file upload completes successfully
[DOM] Dependent child section hides again when uploaded file is removed
[STATE] File upload status drives child visibility (presence of file = has value)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] File upload API returns 200 before child section reveal is triggered
[CONSOLE] no JS errors on file upload or removal; no child-section errors during visibility transitions
Failure_Signals: child section appears before file upload completes; child section fails to hide when file is removed; child section never appears even after successful file upload (VCST-4713 File parent regression); file upload spinner persists after completion
Cleanup: Delete uploaded test file if not removed during test; navigate away from PDP

Title: VCST-4713: Conditional Section Hidden at Cart Submit — configurationItems Excludes Hidden Section
Section: Configurable Products > Conditional Sections > Cart Submission
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
- Product has parent section (A) with dependent child section (B)
- A is configured with a non-None option, making B visible; B has an option selected
- Then A is reset to None, hiding B
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to the configurable product PDP at {{productUrl}}
[ACT] Select a non-None option in parent section A — child section B becomes visible
[ACT] Select an option in child section B
[ASSERT] Verify B is visible and configured
[ACT] Reset parent section A to None — child section B hides
[ASSERT] Verify child section B is now hidden
[ACT] browser_click "Add to Cart"
[WAIT] Wait for cart to update
[ASSERT] browser_snapshot of cart item detail
[ASSERT] Verify configurationItems in cart does NOT include section B option
Assertions:
[STATE] configurationItems in cart addItem mutation excludes hidden section B values (clearHiddenSectionValues() invoked)
[DOM] Cart item shows only section A selection (None); no section B entry in the configured options display
[STATE] Add to Cart succeeds without section B data
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] GraphQL addItem mutation payload does not contain configurationItems entry for section B; addItem returns 200 with no errors[]
[CONSOLE] no JS errors; clearHiddenSectionValues() invoked before cart submission (per implementation)
Failure_Signals: hidden section B value present in cart configurationItems (VCST-4713 cart payload regression); Add to Cart fails with validation error referencing hidden section; cart shows stale option B data from before parent was reset to None
Cleanup: Remove product from cart after test

Title: VCST-4713: Text Section as Parent — Dependent Section Appears When Custom Text Is Entered
Section: Configurable Products > Conditional Sections > Text Parent
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
- Product has a Text-type parent section with a dependent child section
- Text section is set to Custom input mode
- Child section is configured with parentSectionId pointing to Text section
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to the configurable product PDP at {{productUrl}}
[ASSERT] Verify Text parent section is visible with an empty input
[ASSERT] Verify dependent child section is hidden (input is empty)
[ACT] browser_click on the Text section input field
[ACT] browser_type {{testText}} into the Text section input
[WAIT] Wait for conditional visibility to update (debounce if applicable)
[ASSERT] browser_snapshot to confirm child section became visible
[ASSERT] Verify dependent child section is now rendered with its option inputs
[ACT] Clear the text input (select-all and delete)
[ASSERT] Verify dependent child section hides again when text is empty
Assertions:
[DOM] Dependent child section hidden when Text parent input is empty
[DOM] Dependent child section visible after custom text is entered
[DOM] Child section hides again when text input is cleared
[STATE] Text entry (any non-empty value) counts as "has value" for conditional logic
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] no JS errors during text input or clear; no extra API calls triggered by typing
[NETWORK] No page reload on text input
Failure_Signals: child section never appears even after text entry (VCST-4713 Text parent regression); child section appears immediately on page load before any text entered; clearing text does not hide child section; debounce delay makes child appear/hide erratically
Cleanup: Clear text input; navigate away from PDP

Title: Character Counter Updates Live as User Types (mid-range BVA at 5 and 15 chars)
Section: Configurable Products > Text Section > Character Counter
Preconditions:
[PRE:CLEAR_SESSION]
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
- Product with Text section configured with maxLength=30 available on storefront (e.g. @td(CFG_RING.url))
- Text section input is empty; character counter shows 0/30
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to @td(CFG_RING.url)
[ACT] Click into the Text section input with maxLength=30
[ACT] Type exactly 5 characters (e.g. "Hello")
[ASSERT] Verify counter updates to "5 / 30" with no perceptible delay (< 100ms visually)
[ACT] Type 10 more characters (total 15 chars)
[ASSERT] Verify counter updates to "15 / 30"
Assertions:
[DOM] Counter updates to "5 / 30" after typing 5 chars
[DOM] Counter updates to "15 / 30" after typing 15 chars total
[STATE] Counter is reactive — updates with each keystroke (no debounce)
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] No JS errors while typing
Failure_Signals: Counter does not update live (requires blur/submit); counter shows stale value; counter off by one; JS error while typing

Title: Character Counter Shows Visual Emphasis at Exactly maxLength (cap=30 BVA boundary)
Section: Configurable Products > Text Section > Character Counter
Preconditions:
[PRE:CLEAR_SESSION]
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
- On PDP with Text section maxLength=30
Test_Data:
product_url: @td(CFG_RING.url)
max_length: 30
type_text: exactly 30 characters (e.g. ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ1234)
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to @td(CFG_RING.url)
[ACT] Click into the Text section input
[ACT] Type exactly 30 characters
[ASSERT] Verify counter shows "30 / 30"
[ASSERT] Verify counter applies a visual emphasis class (color change to warning or danger token)
[ACT] Attempt to type one more character (31st char)
[ASSERT] Verify the 31st character is NOT accepted (input stays at 30 chars)
[ASSERT] Verify counter still shows "30 / 30" (does not go to "31 / 30")
Assertions:
[DOM] Counter shows "30 / 30" at cap
[DOM] Counter has visual emphasis (different color/class) at 30/30 vs 0/30
[STATE] Input accepts exactly 30 characters, blocks the 31st
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] No JS errors at cap; aria-live announcement for character limit reached may fire
Failure_Signals: Counter shows "31 / 30" or goes above max; no visual emphasis at cap; extra characters accepted beyond maxLength; aria-live not fired

Title: Character Counter at BVA Boundary — maxLength=100: accepts 100 chars, blocks 101st
Section: Configurable Products > Text Section > Character Counter
Preconditions:
[PRE:CLEAR_SESSION]
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
- Product with optional Text section maxLength=100 accessible on storefront (@td(CFG_GIFTBOX.url))
- Text section input is empty; counter shows "0 / 100"
Test_Data:
product_url: @td(CFG_GIFTBOX.url)
max_length: 100
type_text_100: exactly 100 characters (e.g. repeat "A" x100)
type_text_extra: one additional character ("B") for the 101st
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to @td(CFG_GIFTBOX.url)
[ASSERT] Verify character counter shows "0 / 100" in the optional Text section
[ACT] Click into the Text section input
[ACT] Type exactly 100 characters
[ASSERT] Verify counter shows "100 / 100"
[ASSERT] Verify counter applies visual emphasis class (color change to warning or danger token at cap)
[ACT] Attempt to type one more character (101st)
[ASSERT] Verify the 101st character is NOT accepted — input stays at 100 chars
[ASSERT] Verify counter still shows "100 / 100" (does not advance to "101 / 100")
Assertions:
[DOM] Counter shows "0 / 100" when input is empty
[DOM] Counter shows "100 / 100" at cap — BVA upper boundary confirmed
[DOM] Counter has visual emphasis (different color/class) at 100/100 vs 0/100
[STATE] Input accepts exactly 100 characters, blocks the 101st (BVA over-boundary = 101, must be rejected)
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] No TypeError or BVA boundary errors; no null reference errors
Failure_Signals: Counter shows "101 / 100" or accepts extra characters beyond maxLength; no visual emphasis at cap; counter missing or showing wrong max (e.g. 255 instead of 100); counter shows "0 / 0" or NaN

Title: File Section - Selecting None After Uploading Files Clears All Files
Section: Configurable Products > File Section > None Clears Files
Preconditions:
[PRE:CLEAR_SESSION]
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
- Product with optional File section exists (e.g. @td(CFG_FILE_HOODIE.url))
- File uploads enabled
Test_Data:
product_url: @td(CFG_FILE_HOODIE.fullUrl_template)
test_files: 2 small valid files (e.g. .jpg or .png <= 1MB each)
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to the configurable product PDP with an optional File section
[ASSERT] Verify None option is selected by default and file list is empty
[ACT] Click the file upload option (not None)
[ACT] Upload 2 files using the file picker
[ASSERT] Verify 2 files appear in the uploaded files list
[ACT] Click the None radio/option to switch back to None
[ASSERT] Verify all uploaded files are cleared from the file list
[ASSERT] Verify the file upload button/area is no longer visible or is reset
Assertions:
[STATE] Selecting None after file upload clears all files from the file list
[DOM] File list empty after selecting None
[DOM] Upload button/area hidden or reset after selecting None
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] No JS errors when clearing files via None selection
[NETWORK] No file upload requests fired after None selected
Failure_Signals: Files persist after clicking None; file count badge does not reset; JS error on None selection; upload area still shows uploaded files
Cleanup: Remove uploaded files if test abandoned mid-way

Title: File Section - None/Upload State Cycle Repeatable (None clears files on each cycle)
Section: Configurable Products > File Section > None Clears Files
Preconditions:
[PRE:CLEAR_SESSION]
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
- Product with optional File section and None option is accessible on storefront
- File section is in initial state: None selected, no files uploaded (start of fresh PDP visit)
Test_Data:
product_url: @td(CFG_FILE_HOODIE.fullUrl_template)
test_files: 1 small valid file (use different file on second upload)
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to the configurable product PDP with optional File section
[ASSERT] Verify starting state: None selected, no files
[ACT] Switch to file upload mode; upload 1 file
[ASSERT] Verify 1 file in list
[ACT] Click None again
[ASSERT] Verify file list cleared
[ACT] Switch to file upload mode again; upload 1 different file
[ASSERT] Verify 1 new file in list (not the previous file)
[ACT] Click None one final time
[ASSERT] Verify file list cleared again (second cycle)
Assertions:
[STATE] File list cleared each time None is selected (repeatable, not one-time)
[DOM] Switching back to file upload starts with empty list
[DOM] Second None selection also clears
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] No JS errors across multiple None cycles
Failure_Signals: Files accumulate across None/Upload cycles; second None selection does not clear; file from first upload reappears on second switch to upload mode

Title: File Upload Button Disabled When Max Files (5) Reached (BVA at boundary)
Section: Configurable Products > File Section > Max File Limit
Preconditions:
[PRE:CLEAR_SESSION]
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
- Configurable product with File section that allows up to 5 files
- 5 small valid test files available
Test_Data:
product_url: @td(CFG_FILE_HOODIE.fullUrl_template)
max_files: 5
test_files: 5 small valid files (file1.jpg through file5.jpg)
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to configurable product PDP with File section
[ACT] Upload files one by one: upload file1, file2, file3, file4 (4 files total)
[ASSERT] After 4 files: verify upload button is still enabled (max not yet reached)
[ACT] Upload the 5th file
[ASSERT] After 5 files: verify upload button has disabled attribute
[ASSERT] Verify upload button has CSS class vc-file-picker--disabled or equivalent disabled styling
[ASSERT] Verify file count shows 5 / 5 or equivalent max-reached indicator

[DOM] Upload button disabled after 5th file (disabled attribute present)
[DOM] Upload button has vc-file-picker--disabled CSS class at file count = 5
[STATE] Upload button enabled at count 4, disabled at count 5
[DOM] File counter reads 5 / 5 or max reached indicator

Failure_Signals: Upload button still enabled after 5 files; disabled state does not apply at max; disabled CSS class absent; additional file can be added above max

Title: File Upload Button Re-Enabled After Deleting One File from Max (5 files to 4)

Title: Default Option Pre-Selected on PDP Load — Product-Type Section (VCST-4715)
Section: Configurable Products > Default Option > PDP Load
Preconditions: Storefront accessible at {{FRONT_URL}}. Seeded product CFG-030 (@td(CFG_WITH_DEFAULT.name)) is deployed, indexed, and linked to B2B virtual catalog. Product has a Product-type section with exactly one option marked isDefault=true (Carbon, $200 surcharge; base price $100). Registered customer account authenticated.
Steps:
[NAV] Sign in at {{FRONT_URL}} with {{USER_EMAIL}} / {{USER_PASSWORD}}
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}@td(CFG_WITH_DEFAULT.url)
[WAIT] Configure the parameters widget visible; price displayed
[ASSERT] Locate the Product-type section that has a default option configured
[ASSERT] Verify the default option (@td(CFG_WITH_DEFAULT.default_option_name)) is visually selected (radio button active / accordion chip filled) WITHOUT the user clicking anything
[ASSERT] Verify price displayed = @td(CFG_WITH_DEFAULT.price) + @td(CFG_WITH_DEFAULT.default_option_price)
[ASSERT] Verify no other option in this section is simultaneously pre-selected
[ACT] Hard-refresh the page (F5)
[WAIT] Page fully reloads
[ASSERT] Verify default option is still pre-selected after refresh
Assertions:
[STATE] Default option is selected on initial page load without user interaction
[DOM] Default option radio or chip shows selected / active state
[MATH] Displayed price = base_price + default_option_price (default surcharge included from load)
[STATE] Pre-selection sourced from productConfiguration.options[].isDefault=true
[STATE] After page refresh: same default option pre-selected
[STATE] No other option in the same section is pre-selected
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] productConfiguration GraphQL query returns options[].isDefault=true on the expected option; options[].isDefault=false on all others in same section
[CONSOLE] No JavaScript errors on page load or refresh
[NETWORK] No 4xx/5xx on product configuration query
Failure_Signals: Default option NOT pre-selected on load; wrong option pre-selected; base price displayed instead of base+default surcharge; isDefault field absent in GQL response; multiple options showing as selected; pre-selection lost after refresh
References: VCST-4715; Synced: PR #880 vc-module-catalog + PR #115 vc-module-x-cart (2026-05-27)

Title: Default Option NOT Pre-Selected When Product Has No Default Configured — Backward Compatibility (VCST-4715)
Section: Configurable Products > Default Option > Backward Compat
Preconditions: Storefront accessible at {{FRONT_URL}}. Product with NO isDefault option configured (@td(CFG_BIKE.name)) exists. This is the backward-compatibility guard — isDefault defaults to false and must not accidentally pre-select options. Registered customer account authenticated.
Steps:
[NAV] Sign in at {{FRONT_URL}} with {{USER_EMAIL}} / {{USER_PASSWORD}}
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}@td(CFG_BIKE.url)
[WAIT] Configure the parameters widget visible
[ASSERT] Verify None is selected by default across all Product-type sections (no option pre-selected)
[ASSERT] Verify price displayed = base price only (no surcharge added from phantom default)
[ACT] Hard-refresh the page
[WAIT] Page fully reloads
[ASSERT] Still: None selected; price = base price only
Assertions:
[STATE] No option is pre-selected on first load and after refresh
[MATH] Displayed price = base price only; no default surcharge phantom-added
[STATE] All options[].isDefault=false in productConfiguration response
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] productConfiguration query returns all options[].isDefault=false for @td(CFG_BIKE.name); no option has isDefault=true
[CONSOLE] No JavaScript errors
[NETWORK] No 4xx/5xx
Failure_Signals: Any option pre-selected on a product with no isDefault configured; base price altered by phantom default surcharge, isDefault=true returned for unconfigured option

Title: User Override of Pre-Selected Default Option — Selecting Different Option (VCST-4715)
Section: Configurable Products > Default Option > User Override
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
Storefront accessible. Seed product @td(CFG_WITH_DEFAULT.name) with default option pre-selected. Cart empty. User authenticated.
Steps:
[NAV] Sign in; navigate to @td(CFG_WITH_DEFAULT.name) PDP
[WAIT] Configure widget visible; default option pre-selected; price reflects default surcharge
[ASSERT] Confirm default option (@td(CFG_WITH_DEFAULT.default_option_name)) is pre-selected
[ACT] Click a DIFFERENT option in the same section (non-default)
[WAIT] Selection changes; price updates
[ASSERT] Verify non-default option is now selected (radio active)
[ASSERT] Verify default option is now de-selected (radio inactive)
[ASSERT] Verify price = base + non-default option surcharge (NOT base + default surcharge)
[ACT] Click Add to Cart
[WAIT] Success notification
[NAV] Navigate to cart
[ASSERT] Cart line item shows NON-DEFAULT option in configuration details
Assertions:
[STATE] User override works: selecting a different option de-selects the default
[STATE] Only one option selected at a time (single-select enforced)
[MATH] Cart price = base + non-default option surcharge
[DOM] Cart line item configuration shows non-default option name
[STATE] Default is a UI convenience — user can always change it
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] addItem configurationItems[] references the NON-DEFAULT option id
[CONSOLE] No JS errors during selection change
[NETWORK] No 4xx/5xx
Failure_Signals: Default option stays highlighted after user picks different option; cart price uses default surcharge; addItem sends default option id despite user choosing differently; JS error on deselect
References: VCST-4715; Synced: PR #880 vc-module-catalog + PR #115 vc-module-x-cart (2026-05-27)

Title: Default Option in Dependent (Conditional) Section Pre-Selected When Section Becomes Visible (VCST-4715)
Section: Configurable Products > Default Option > Conditional Section Default
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
Storefront accessible. Seeded conditional product CFG-031 (@td(CFG_WITH_DEFAULT_COND.name)): parent Product section Base Choice (required, default=Standard $0) reveals dependent section Add-on (dependsOnSectionId=parent) whose option Warranty ($25) has isDefault=true. Because the parent carries a default, the dependent section is visible on PDP load. User authenticated. Base price $150.
Steps:
[NAV] Sign in; navigate to @td(CFG_WITH_DEFAULT_COND.url)
[WAIT] Configure widget visible; parent section Base Choice shows its default (Standard) preselected
[ASSERT] Because the parent carries a value, the dependent section Add-on IS visible on load
[ASSERT] Verify the default option (Warranty) in the dependent section is pre-selected WITHOUT clicking it
[ASSERT] Total reflects base + parent default + dependent default surcharge (base $150 + Standard $0 + Warranty $25 = $175 with seeded values)
[ACT] Change the parent to a non-default option; verify the dependent section stays visible and its default remains applied
[ACT] Click Add to Cart
[WAIT] Cart mutation completes
Assertions:
[STATE] Dependent section visible on load (parent carries a default); its default option auto-selected without user interaction
[MATH] Total = base + parent default surcharge + dependent default surcharge (assert structurally if catalog prices drift)
[DOM] Dependent default option shows selected/active state in its accordion
[STATE] Dependent default preselection holds when the parent value changes
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] addItem configurationItems[] includes entry for the dependent section with default option id
[CONSOLE] No JS errors during section reveal or auto-selection
[NETWORK] No 4xx/5xx
Failure_Signals: Dependent section appears with no option pre-selected despite default configured; price does not include dependent section default surcharge; addItem omits dependent section default from configurationItems
